response:
The probability that a student from Group B exceeds Group A requires analyzing the difference between two normal distributions. Let D equal X_B minus X_A. The mean of D is 75 minus 70 which equals 5. The variance of D is the sum of the individual variances so 10 squared plus 12 squared equals 244. The standard deviation of D is the square root of 244 which is approximately 15.62. We seek the probability that D is greater than zero. The Z score is 0 minus 5 divided by 15.62 which equals roughly -0.32. The area to the right of this Z score is approximately 0.6255. This calculation provides an explanation for the likelihood of higher engagement in the new curriculum.

To find when performance levels match we must solve the equation where 50 times e to the power of 0.02t equals 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of -0.15 times t minus 5. There is no simple algebraic force that yields an exact closed form solution for this transcendental equation. The process involves numerical methods or graphing to locate the intersection point. Nothing prevents us from estimating the value through iteration. At t equals 10 the left side is about 61 and the right side is about 73. At t equals 15 the left side is about 74 and the right side is about 88. At t equals 20 the left side is about 90 and the right side is about 96. The curves appear to converge near t equals 22 or 23. A more precise check shows they are very close around t equals 22.5. This time value represents the moment both groups share the same performance improvement level.
